perturbation.original_text: ii) Recipient: (1) Shall not disclose Confide ntial Information to any employe e or subcontractor of Recipient un less such person needs access in order to facilitate the evaluation and preparation of a response to RFP CSP903918 and executes a nondisclosure agreement with the Recipient with terms no less restrictive tha n those of this Section 2)b) ; (2) Shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other third party without Discloser’s prior written consent ; and (3) Shall not reproduce Confidential Information in any form except as required to facilitate the evaluation and preparation of a response to RFP CSP903918 .
perturbation.changed_text: ii) Recipient: (1) May disclose Confide ntial Information to any employe e or subcontractor of Recipient un less such person needs access in order to facilitate the evaluation and preparation of a response to RFP CSP903918 and executes a nondisclosure agreement with the Recipient with terms no less restrictive tha n those of this Section 2)b) ; (2) Shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other third party without Discloser’s prior written consent ; and (3) Shall not reproduce Confidential Information in any form except as required to facilitate the evaluation and preparation of a response to RFP CSP903918 .
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Shall not disclose Confidential Information' to 'May disclose Confidential Information' introduces a direct contradiction. While (2) maintains a restriction of disclosing to third parties without consent, (1) now permits disclosure to employees/subcontractors under certain conditions. This directly conflicts with the general principle of confidentiality established earlier and creates an uncertainty about what confidential treatment is required overall, therefore, it is contradictory to the other points.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ii) Recipient: (1) May disclose Confide ntial Information to any employe e or subcontractor of Recipient un less such person needs access in order to facilitate the evaluation and preparation of a response to RFP CSP903918 and executes a nondisclosure agreement with the Recipient with terms no less restrictive tha n those of this Section 2)b) ; (2) Shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other third party without Discloser’s prior written consent ; and (3) Shall not reproduce Confidential Information in any form except as required to facilitate the evaluation and preparation of a response to RFP CSP903918 .
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the recipient "Shall not disclose Confidential Information" to employees/subcontractors, while the changed text states that the recipient "May disclose Confide ntial Information" to employees/subcontractors under certain conditions. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. The word change from 'shall not' to 'may' introduces a direct contradiction, as the original imposed a restriction on disclosing confidential information, which is lifted, allowing for disclosure in certain circumstances and directly contradicting the implied confidentiality elsewhere.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient affirms that it sha ll not and shall not allow others to take the Discloser’s Confidential Information outside the United States without express written authorization from the Discloser.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient affirms that it sha ll use its best efforts to not allow others to take the Discloser’s Confidential Information outside the United States without express written authorization from the Discloser.
perturbation.explanation: The modification from 'shall not' to 'shall use its best efforts to not' creates ambiguity regarding the prohibition of taking confidential information outside the United States. The original version is a strict prohibition, whereas the modified text implies a weaker obligation, allowing the recipient some leeway if 'best efforts' are made but the information still leaves the country. The in-text contradiction is the shift from a defined duty to a discretionary effort, directly affecting the enforceability and interpretation of this clause. It's contradictory when another sections within the agreement states that the Recipient is liable for disclosure of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient will be liable for disclosure of any Confidential Information. The Parties agree tha t the disclosure of Confidential Information of the Discloser may cause the Discloser irreparable damage for which remedies other than injunctive relief may be inadequate, and the Recipient agrees that in the event of a breach of the obligations hereunder, t he Discloser shall be entitled to temporary and permanent injunctive relief to enforce this provision without the necessity of proving actual damages. This provision shall not, however, diminish or alter any right to claim and recover damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibits taking confidential information outside the United States. The changed text weakens this obligation by requiring only 'best efforts' to prevent such action. This creates a direct contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text' section, which states that the recipient 'will be liable for disclosure of any Confidential Information.' The 'best efforts' clause introduces ambiguity and potential exceptions to this liability, making the contract inconsistent and potentially unenforceable because the Recipient could claim they used their best efforts to prevent disclosure and, therefore, should not be liable despite the Confidential Information being disclosed.
